approach: Mandatory source/literature baseline followed by a scoped Lean interface draft and bounded independent semantic controls

strategy.mechanism: Translate the maintained min-max statement using Finset, Nat.findGreatest, and Mathlib asymptotic equivalence; test the Sidon semantics independently before repository-pinned elaboration.

hypothesis: The proposed four-variable Sidon predicate represents uniqueness of unordered pair sums with repetition, and the resulting bounded extremal interface can express the explicit conjecture ell(N) ~ sqrt(N).
discriminating_test: Compare the four-variable predicate with an independently implemented unordered-pair-sum encoding on every subset of {-4,...,4}, then attempt Lean elaboration far enough to identify whether the local environment contains the required repository dependencies.
summary: The mandatory baseline was completed. A May 2026 preprint postdating the official page's last edit was identified and incorporated. A Lean candidate defines IsSidon, GuaranteedSidonSize, ell via Nat.findGreatest, maximality API lemmas, three controls, and the answer(sorry)-scoped asymptotic declaration. Two independent semantic encodings agreed on all 512 subsets of {-4,...,4}; all seven adversarial controls passed. The Lean candidate was not elaborated because this environment lacks FormalConjectures and Mathlib. Two low-memory diagnostics also failed while creating a Lean thread; a controlled 4096 MB single-thread run reached the actual unknown-module error.
rationale: The source and current literature audit identifies a legitimate unfilled formalization target, and the bounded checker rejects the two most likely semantic mistranslations. However, finite semantic agreement is not kernel validation, and no repository build, CI, or human statement review has occurred. Progress rather than candidate status is therefore warranted.
claims: As accessed on 2026-07-20, Erdős Problem #530 is marked open, asks whether ell(N) ~ sqrt(N), and reports no formalised statement.

Bailleul and Riblet's arXiv:2605.03181v1 reports the lower bound (1/(3 sqrt 3)+o(1)) sqrt(n) for arbitrary finite subsets of the integers and reals, without reaching the conjectured leading constant 1.
The literal four-variable Sidon predicate and unordered-pair uniqueness agree on every subset of {-4,...,4}; this is a finite computed fact only.
FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/530.lean is a drafted, unelaborated candidate and is not yet a completed formalization.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-194758-ee6344/stdout.txt: 512 subsets, 140 Sidon subsets, zero mismatches, seven controls true
next_steps: In a network-enabled checkout of google-deepmind/formal-conjectures, copy the candidate file and run lake build FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.530 at the repository-pinned Lean 4.27.0 and Mathlib commit.
If the target build passes, run the full lake build and inspect that sorry occurs only in the answer placeholder and open conjecture proof.
Request assignment on issue #773 and obtain human review of repeated summands, swapped-pair triviality, exact-size guarantees, Nat.findGreatest maximality, and the ~[atTop]/Real.sqrt translation.
Resolve the literature-note discrepancy between Abbott's abstract constant c < 2/25 and the 2026 preprint's description of the prior constant as 0.0805.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-194758-ee6344: successful semantic comparison on all 512 subsets of {-4,...,4}
.proof-experiments/20260720-194849-f1733c: failed resource control at 256 MB; Lean could not create a thread
.proof-experiments/20260720-194914-3f029d: failed resource control at 2048 MB; Lean could not create a thread
.proof-experiments/20260720-194925-0f2242: with LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1 and 4096 MB, reached the decisive missing FormalConjectures module error
independent_checker: checks/check_sidon_semantics.py uses unordered pairs with repetition, materially different from the candidate's four-variable implication. It exhaustively compared both encodings on all subsets of {-4,...,4}. It does not elaborate Lean or validate the asymptotic statement.
transfer_insights: Mathlib's Ruzsa-Szemeredi extremal-number definition uses Nat.findGreatest -> prediction: the same bounded idiom gives a short maximality API for ell -> drafted successfully but repository elaboration remains pending.
Weak-Sidon literature excludes repeated summands -> prediction: that encoding will miss 0+2=1+1 in {0,1,2} -> observed, so the transfer was rejected for Problem #530.

established_facts.claim: The two exact finite Sidon encodings agree on every subset of {-4,...,4}.
established_facts.scope: Exactly 512 subsets of the nine-element integer domain

established_facts.claim: The maintained source marks Problem #530 open and not formalised.
established_facts.scope: Maintained-site status, which explicitly disclaims literature completeness

established_facts.claim: The May 2026 preprint reports a 1/(3 sqrt 3) asymptotic lower-bound constant over arbitrary finite subsets of R^d.
established_facts.scope: Claim of arXiv v1; proof not independently reconstructed this epoch

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Formalize weak Sidon uniqueness while ignoring repeated summands.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: An authoritative source explicitly changes the target to weak Sidon sets.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Demand uniqueness of ordered pair representations.
ruled_out.reason: It wrongly rejects swapped representations (a,b) and (b,a).
ruled_out.reopen_condition: None for the present source statement.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the official page's April 2026 literature summary as current-complete.
ruled_out.reason: arXiv:2605.03181 was submitted after the page edit and improves the lower-bound constant.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Not applicable; future audits must search beyond the page edit date.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Increase the finite checker cutoff as a contribution.
ruled_out.reason: A larger arbitrary cutoff would neither validate Lean nor advance the mathematical bound.
ruled_out.evidence: The successful 512-case test already meets its control purpose.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Only a newly identified semantic ambiguity requiring a specifically targeted finite control.

open_leads.description: Pinned Lean elaboration and full repository build
open_leads.rationale: This is the cheapest decisive verification of the drafted artifact.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run the exact build command recorded in CHECKPOINT.md inside a current Formal Conjectures checkout.

open_leads.rationale: Kernel checking cannot establish that the declaration faithfully translates the English source.
open_leads.next_experiment: Request issue #773 assignment and submit a linked PR with the semantic-review checklist.

open_leads.rationale: The abstract says c < 2/25 while the new preprint reports 0.0805; propagating an unexplained value would weaken the audit.
open_leads.next_experiment: Compare the relevant proof optimization and exact citation passage in the two papers.

blocker: The current workspace has Lean 4.32.0 but no Formal Conjectures or Mathlib checkout, shell network access cannot resolve GitHub, and the target repository pins Lean 4.27.0 with Mathlib commit a3a10db0e9d66acbebf76c5e6a135066525ac900. No kernel build or external review is therefore available.
reopen_condition: A network-enabled or prepopulated current Formal Conjectures checkout at the pinned dependency versions, followed by target and full builds.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Provide a precise Lean declaration of Erdős Problem #530's largest guaranteed Sidon-subset function and the conjecture ell(N) ~ sqrt(N).
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The open Formal Conjectures issue currently has no linked implementation; the workspace adds a bounded extremal definition, maximality API, adversarial controls, and a source-scoped asymptotic declaration, but it remains unbuilt.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: Repository-pinned target build and full lake build succeed, repository CI passes, only the open conjecture remains sorry-backed, and a maintainer approves the informal-to-formal translation.

closest_prior_work.difference: The issue requests the formalization but contains no linked branch or pull request.
This is the closest current mathematical work and improves a lower-bound constant; it is not a Lean formalization and does not prove the conjectured constant.

novelty_searches.finding: Found the directly relevant May 2026 Bailleul-Riblet preprint and the February 2026 weak-Sidon variant; neither supplies a Lean formalization or settles constant 1.

external_channel.recipient: Google DeepMind Formal Conjectures maintainers via issue #773 and a linked pull request
external_channel.acceptance_path: Request assignment, sign the Google CLA, build at pinned versions, submit a linked PR, pass CI, and obtain maintainer review.

continuation.objective: Convert the drafted interface into a kernel-checked, externally reviewed Formal Conjectures contribution.
continuation.first_action: Execute the erdos530-lean-build command in CHECKPOINT.md from a current repository checkout.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop successfully when target and full builds pass and a repository reviewer confirms statement equivalence; otherwise stop at and preserve the first elaboration or semantic discrepancy.

strategy_proposals.mechanism: Give a reviewer only the official source and compiled candidate, asking them to reconstruct the meaning of IsSidon, GuaranteedSidonSize, ell, and the asymptotic declaration without the design narrative.
strategy_proposals.hypothesis: An independent reviewer will recover the same unordered-pair, exact-cardinality, and asymptotic semantics.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: Record the first discrepancy in a pull-request review; absence of a discrepancy is not accepted until the kernel build also passes.
strategy_proposals.rationale: This directly attacks informal-to-formal mismatch, which finite controls and the Lean kernel cannot fully detect.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol principal performed synthesis, source audit, artifact design, and experiment interpretation. One GPT-5.6 Terra source-discriminator delegate supplied an advisory memo whose hash was verified; every relied-on claim was rechecked against sources or deterministic artifacts. Tools used: web search/opening, GitHub and authoritative documentation, Python 3.12.3 exact enumeration, the computational-researcher experiment recorder, shell diagnostics, and Lean 4.32.0 for environment checks. No Mathlib/Formal Conjectures kernel build, CAS, SAT/SMT solver, cloud computation, repository CI, or external expert validation was available.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.
